subject: Weeds out of control at 2218 Bohland Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I've lived next door to 2218 Bohland Ave for eleven years and I've never seen the grass this bad. It's at least three feet tall along the side yard and back fence. My kids play outside and something could be living in there. I reported it on the village website two weeks ago and nothing happened. Can your office please look into this?

subject: Weeds out of control at 2218 Bohland Ave
body_clean: Ms. Wojcik, thank you for reaching out. I inspected 2218 Bohland Ave this morning. The property is in violation of Village Code Section 8-2-4 — vegetation exceeding eight inches. I posted a Notice of Violation today, July 11, giving the owner ten days to cut and remove all overgrown grass. If not corrected by July 21, the Village will arrange abatement and bill the property owner. I'll follow up once compliance is confirmed.

subject: Junk car parked in yard at 1604 Granville Ave
body_clean: Hello Mayor, there is a car with no license plates sitting on flat tires in the front yard at 1604 Granville Ave. It has been there since at least August. The hood is propped open with a stick and there are weeds growing up around the tires. I walk by this on my way to the Metra every morning. It's making the whole block look bad.

subject: Junk car parked in yard at 1604 Granville Ave
body_clean: Mr. Mbeki, I inspected 1604 Granville Ave on October 9. The vehicle is an inoperable 2003 Chevy Cavalier, unplated, stored on unpaved yard space in violation of Village Code Section 11-5-2. A Notice of Violation was issued today giving the owner 14 days to remove or properly enclose the vehicle. Failure to comply will result in a citation and possible referral for towing. I'll note your concern in the file.

subject: Foreclosed property on Harvard Ave is a disaster
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, the house at 711 Harvard Ave has been empty for over a year. The grass in front is knee-high, there are two broken windows on the second floor, and the gutters are hanging off the fascia. Neighbors have started seeing rats around the property. I know it's a bank property but surely the village can do something. Who do I even contact about this?

subject: Foreclosed property on Harvard Ave is a disaster
body_clean: Ms. Reyes, thank you for bringing this to my attention. I've forwarded your email to Code Enforcement Officer Sandra Pulaski, who handles foreclosed and vacant properties. She'll inspect 711 Harvard Ave and initiate contact with the mortgage holder of record. We take these situations seriously — a neglected vacant structure affects the whole neighborhood. You should hear from Sandra within a few business days.

subject: Fence falling apart at 2509 Morris Ave
body_clean: Mayor, the wooden privacy fence at 2509 Morris Ave is leaning so far that several boards have actually fallen into my yard. I've spoken to the owner twice and she says she'll fix it but nothing has happened in three months. The fence is rotted at the base and my dog got out twice because of the gaps. I'm at 2511 Morris Ave if anyone needs to see it.

subject: Fence falling apart at 2509 Morris Ave
body_clean: Mr. Hutchins, I visited both properties on August 14. The fence at 2509 Morris Ave is in violation of Section 10-3-7 — structural failure, leaning past the property line and presenting an encroachment. A notice was delivered to the owner today with a 21-day compliance window to repair or replace the damaged sections. If not corrected by September 4, I'll issue a formal citation. I've documented the gaps and the fallen boards.

subject: Rats coming from vacant lot on 22nd Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm writing about the vacant lot on 22nd Ave between Madison and Monroe. It's become completely overgrown and we have been dealing with a serious rat problem for two months. My neighbor set four traps in her garage and caught three rats in one week. The lot is full of debris, old lumber, and what looks like a mattress frame. This is a health issue, not just a nuisance. Please help.

subject: Rats coming from vacant lot on 22nd Ave
body_clean: Ms. Szymanski, the mayor's office forwarded your concern and I inspected the lot on September 19. I confirmed overgrown vegetation and debris in violation of Sections 8-2-4 and 8-4-1. The property owner has been issued a notice requiring vegetation clearing and full debris removal within 14 days, by October 3. I'm also flagging this for a Cook County pest control referral given the rodent activity you described. Please reach back out if you see no improvement by mid-October.

subject: Tall weeds blocking sightlines at 50th and Washington
body_clean: Hi Mayor, wanted to flag something becoming a safety issue. The corner lot at 50th Ave and Washington Blvd — around 215 or 217 50th Ave — has weeds growing so tall along the sidewalk that you can't see oncoming traffic when pulling out of the alley. I almost got T-boned last Tuesday morning. The lot looks unoccupied. Can something be done quickly?

subject: Overgrown lot next to my house on Englewood Ave
body_clean: Good morning Mayor Okonkwo. I hope this finds you well. I am writing about the empty lot at 1902 Englewood Ave that sits directly beside my home. Every spring the weeds and brush grow back worse than the year before, and this year there is also a large pile of broken concrete that appeared over the winter. I am concerned about snakes nesting there as the season gets hotter. The lot has been empty for at least four years. What can be done?

subject: Multiple inoperable vehicles at 318 Hirsch Ave
body_clean: Mayor, the property at 318 Hirsch Ave has four cars in the backyard and two more on the side of the house. None have plates or move. One is sitting on cinder blocks with no wheels. The owner appears to be dismantling them — there are engine parts and fluid stains on the driveway. It looks like an unlicensed auto repair operation. I have photos if that helps. I'm at 316 Hirsch Ave.

subject: Abandoned house at 408 St. Paul Ave needs attention
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I own 410 St. Paul Ave and the house next door at 408 has been empty since the family moved out in spring. By September the lawn was already out of control. Now there are enormous leaf piles mixed with junk and someone broke a side window last month. Nobody seems to be watching the property. With cold nights coming I'm worried about squatters or a fire. Is the village aware of this one?

subject: Weeds and junk at 2840 Bellwood Ave, ongoing situation
body_clean: Mayor, this is my third time reaching out about 2840 Bellwood Ave in two summers. The weeds are back worse than ever — shoulder-height goldenrod and thistle along the entire front. There is also a broken recliner that's been on the porch since June. I don't want to be that neighbor who calls constantly, but I've talked to the owner and gotten nowhere. The block association president can confirm this is an ongoing pattern.

subject: Weeds and junk at 2840 Bellwood Ave, ongoing situation
body_clean: Ms. Wierzbicki, I hear your frustration and it's completely valid. I reviewed the prior complaint history before visiting today — notices were issued in both 2023 and 2024, one of which went to abatement. Repeat violations allow us to escalate. I've issued a citation this time rather than another courtesy notice — a $150 fine under Section 8-2-4 and mandatory re-inspection in ten days. The porch furniture is cited under Section 8-5-1 as well. I will personally track this one to completion.

subject: Vacant commercial property at 527 Mannheim Rd overgrown
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, the small commercial building at 527 Mannheim Rd has been vacant since last winter. The lot around it is completely overgrown and there are two dumpsters worth of old furniture and construction debris piled against the rear wall. I drive past it every day and it's embarrassing for the community. I understand Mannheim is a state road but the building lot is still village jurisdiction, right? Can code enforcement take a look?

subject: Old pickup truck blocking sidewalk at 1122 Monroe St
body_clean: Hello Mayor. There is a rust-covered pickup truck at 1122 Monroe St — partially on the lawn, partially on the sidewalk. It's been there at least two months with expired plates and flat tires. I've had to push my stroller into the street to get around it three times. There are children on this block. Please have someone check on this.

subject: Old pickup truck blocking sidewalk at 1122 Monroe St
body_clean: Ms. Nwosu, thank you for writing — a sidewalk-blocking vehicle is more than an eyesore; it's an accessibility and safety issue. I've passed this to Sandra Pulaski in Code Enforcement and flagged the sidewalk obstruction angle separately so it gets expedited. We'll have someone out there shortly. Please reach back out if nothing has changed in a week.

subject: Rat problem traced to overgrown lot at 2030 Marshall Ave
body_clean: Mayor, we have a rat problem on our end of Marshall Ave. After talking to four neighbors I'm convinced it's coming from the overgrown lot at 2030 Marshall. It hasn't been maintained in at least two years — tall grass, brush piles, and old appliances buried in the vegetation. We've had rats in garages, garbage cans, and one neighbor even found them in her basement. I'd like a code inspector AND someone from public health out there if possible.

subject: Grass at foreclosed home on 19th Ave not cut all season
body_clean: Mayor, the foreclosed property at 1845 19th Ave has not had its grass cut once this summer. We are in July and it looks like a field. I called 311 back in May and was told it would be addressed. It was not. Is the village going to abate this or not? My property value is directly affected by what happens on that block.

subject: Junk and weeds at 3101 Washington Blvd corner lot
body_clean: Hi Mayor Okonkwo. The corner lot at Frederick Ave and Washington — I believe the address is 3101 Washington Blvd — has been an eyesore since summer. Old tires stacked against the garage, weeds about two feet tall along both sides of the lot, and a beat-up boat trailer that hasn't moved since spring. Is there already pending action on this one or does a complaint need to be filed formally?

subject: Weeds at 1516 Geneva Ave already starting early this year
body_clean: Mayor — I know it's only March but the lot at 1516 Geneva Ave is already showing tall dead weeds from last year mixed with new growth. This property has been a problem the last two summers. I'm getting ahead of it this time to see if reporting early makes a difference. The owner did nothing when notices were sent before. Is there a way to flag repeat-offender properties so enforcement is faster next time?

subject: Weeds at 1516 Geneva Ave already starting early this year
body_clean: Mr. Quigley, your instinct to report early is exactly right. I pulled the file on 1516 Geneva Ave — two prior violations in 2022 and 2023, one of which went to abatement. That history qualifies the property for our repeat-offender monitoring list, which means I'll do a proactive inspection in May without waiting for a complaint and skip the courtesy notice stage if there's a violation. If the vegetation is out of compliance at any point this season, we go straight to citation. I've flagged it in our system.
